summary: This encyclopedic entry outlines the core tenets of Confucianism, an ancient Chinese philosophy focused on personal ethics, morality, and social harmony. It highlights Confucius's emphasis on virtues like ren (humanity), filial piety, the importance of education, and the role of rituals in maintaining a peaceful and structured society.

principles.statement: Do not do unto others what you would not want others to do unto you.
principles.rationale: It is the foundational human-centered virtue for living a peaceful life and interacting ethically with others.
principles.source_quote: do not do unto others what you would not want others to do unto you.

principles.statement: People are essentially good yet may have strayed from the appropriate forms of conduct.
principles.rationale: Because human nature is inherently good, individuals can use education and rituals to correct their behavior and cultivate a virtuous character.
principles.source_quote: He thought that people are essentially good yet may have strayed from the appropriate forms of conduct.

principles.statement: Devotion to family is the foundation that strengthens society.
principles.rationale: The family is the most important group in society; practicing filial piety and submission to parental authority scales up to create a stable, harmonious state.
principles.source_quote: The family is the most important group for Confucian ethics, and devotion to family can only strengthen the society surrounding it.

principles.statement: A leader's moral perfection creates a peaceful and benevolent society.
principles.rationale: A leader with good moral character positively affects the world around them through cosmic harmony, whereas straying from morality causes conflict.
principles.source_quote: If an emperor has moral perfection, his rule will be peaceful and benevolent.

frameworks.name: Cultivation of Ren (Humanity)
frameworks.when_to_apply: When seeking to develop a good moral character and create harmony in one's community.
steps: Recognize that people are essentially good.
Pursue education to create a virtuous character.
Practice rituals to bring about a respectful attitude and create a sense of community.
Exhibit virtuous behaviors such as respect, altruism, and humility.
frameworks.source_quote: This moral character is achieved through the virtue of ren, or “humanity,” which leads to more virtuous behaviors, such as respect, altruism and humility.

mental_models.name: Cosmic Harmony
mental_models.description: The lens that personal moral character directly influences the external physical and political world. Virtuous leadership yields peace, while moral decay manifests as physical and societal disasters.
mental_models.example: If an emperor has moral perfection, his rule will be peaceful and benevolent. Natural disasters and conflict, on the other hand, are a natural consequence of straying from ancient teachings.

mental_models.name: Family as the State
mental_models.description: Using family structures, devotion, and hierarchy as a metaphor and blueprint for organizing the government and society at large.
mental_models.example: The use of family metaphors, such as “son of heaven,” to describe the emperor and his government.

heuristics: Stay in your social class to create social harmony.
Look to traditional knowledge and ancient thinkers to maintain societal stability.

signature_quotes.text: do not do unto others what you would not want others to do unto you.
signature_quotes.context: Described as the golden rule of Confucianism.

anti_patterns.description: Straying from ancient teachings and appropriate forms of conduct.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It disrupts cosmic harmony, leading as a natural consequence to natural disasters and societal conflict.

anti_patterns.description: Pursuing luxury and wealth over moral character.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Confucius argued against luxury, believing it disrupted social harmony, which is why merchants were placed lowest on the social scale.
